description: Contractually agreed annual percentage increase applied to the PMPM rate at each rate renewal period (e.g., 3.50 for 3.5% annual escalation). Used for multi-year capitation arrangements.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Date on which the capitation arrangement was formally approved by the health plan's contracting authority. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: Name or employee identifier of the contracting authority who approved this capitation arrangement. Supports audit trail and delegation-of-authority compliance.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; SOC 2 Type II Audit Requirements

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the capitation arrangement (e.g., 'Primary Care Capitation - HMO Commercial 2024'), used in reporting and provider communications.

Externally-known business identifier for the capitation arrangement, used in provider communications, payment remittances, and reconciliation processes. Assigned by the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Current lifecycle status of the capitation arrangement. Controls whether PMPM payments are generated and whether members can be attributed to this arrangement.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Classification of the capitation arrangement by scope of covered services. Global capitation covers all services; primary care capitation covers PCP services only; specialty capitation covers specialist services; institutional covers facility-based care. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global_capitation|primary_care_capitation|specialty_capitation|institutional_capitation|behavioral_health_capitation|pharmacy_capitation — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Current number of members attributed to this capitation arrangement for the active payment period. Used to calculate total monthly capitation payment disbursement.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NCQA Attribution Methodology Standards

description: Methodology used to attribute members to this capitation arrangement. PCP assignment uses enrollment data; claims-based uses plurality of visits; voluntary selection allows member choice; geographic assigns by service area.
reference: CMS Medicare Shared Savings Program Attribution Rules; NCQA PCMH Standards

description: Indicates whether this capitation arrangement automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides notice of termination within the contractually specified notice period.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; State DOI Contract Filing Requirements

description: Description of services explicitly excluded from the capitation scope and reimbursed separately (e.g., 'Mental Health, Substance Abuse, Transplants, Neonatal ICU'). Carve-outs are paid fee-for-service or under separate capitation arrangements.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Timestamp when this capitation arrangement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance requirements.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312 Audit Controls; SOC 2 Type II; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Date on which the capitation arrangement becomes binding and PMPM payments begin. Aligns with the contract effective period.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Description or code of the geographic service area (county, state, or region) within which this capitation arrangement applies. Members outside this area may not be attributed to this arrangement.
reference: CMS Service Area Requirements 42 CFR Part 422.2; State DOI Service Area Filings

description: Dollar amount per member per year above which the health plan assumes financial responsibility for claims costs, protecting the provider from catastrophic individual member costs. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Stop-Loss Model Act

description: Line of Business to which this capitation arrangement applies (e.g., Commercial HMO, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid Managed Care). Determines regulatory requirements and rate-setting methodology.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Regulations 42 CFR Part 422; CMS Medicaid Managed Care 42 CFR Part 438; ACA Section 1301

description: Frequency at which capitation payments are disbursed to the provider. Monthly is standard for most capitation arrangements; quarterly or annual may apply for certain specialty or institutional arrangements.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Specific payment calculation methodology applied to this capitation arrangement. Pure capitation pays flat PMPM; withhold arrangements retain a percentage pending performance; risk-adjusted capitation applies RAF multipliers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pure_capitation|capitation_with_withhold|capitation_with_bonus|risk_adjusted_capitation|tiered_capitation — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Risk Adjustment 42 CFR Part 422.308; NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications

description: Fixed dollar amount paid to the provider per attributed member per calendar month, regardless of services rendered. The core financial parameter of the capitation arrangement. Expressed in USD.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Actuarial Standards

business_glossary_term: Per Member Per Month (PMPM) Rate Currency
description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the PMPM rate (typically USD for domestic health plans). Required for multi-currency reporting and regulatory submissions.

Description of the quality performance benchmarks or HEDIS/STAR measure thresholds that must be met for the provider to receive release of withheld capitation amounts. Defines the performance contract terms.
reference: NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS Star Ratings Methodology; CMS Medicare Advantage Quality Bonus Program

description: Multiplier applied to the base PMPM rate to account for the relative health risk of the attributed member population. A RAF above 1.0 indicates a sicker-than-average population; below 1.0 indicates healthier-than-average.
reference: CMS HCC Risk Adjustment Model; CMS RAPS/EDPS Submission Requirements

description: Date on which the current PMPM rate became effective. Supports rate history tracking when rates are renegotiated mid-contract without creating a new arrangement record.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Actuarial Standards

description: Date on which the current PMPM rate expires and is subject to renegotiation or automatic escalation. Null if the rate is open-ended for the arrangement term.
reference: CMS Actuarial Soundness Requirements 42 CFR Part 438.4; NAIC Model Actuarial Standards

description: Indicates whether the PMPM rate is subject to risk adjustment based on the attributed member population's Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) scores. Applicable primarily for Medicare Advantage and ACA Exchange arrangements.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment 42 CFR Part 422.308; HHS HCC Risk Adjustment Methodology

description: Indicates whether the provider participates in a shared risk pool under this capitation arrangement. Risk pool participants share in surpluses or deficits relative to the capitation budget.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Risk-Sharing Standards

description: Percentage of risk pool surplus or deficit allocated to the provider under this capitation arrangement. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Applicable only when risk_pool_participant is True.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Risk-Sharing Standards

description: Comma-delimited list or narrative description of the covered service categories included under this capitation arrangement (e.g., 'Primary Care, Preventive Services, Lab, Radiology'). Defines the scope of services the provider is financially responsible for under capitation.
reference: CMS Essential Health Benefits 45 CFR Part 156; NCQA Accreditation Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this capitation arrangement record was sourced (e.g., FACETS, QNXT for Core Administration Platform; CACTUS or PROVIDER_SOURCE for Provider Management System).
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; EDI HIPAA 834 Transaction Standards

description: Native record identifier from the operational source system (Facets, QNXT, or Provider Management System) for this capitation arrangement. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and back-referencing to the system of record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164 Audit Controls

description: Type of stop-loss protection included in the capitation arrangement. Individual stop-loss limits exposure per member; aggregate stop-loss limits total population exposure; both applies both thresholds.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Stop-Loss Model Act

description: Date on which the capitation arrangement ends and PMPM payments cease. Null for open-ended arrangements. Distinct from contract termination date when partial termination applies.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards

description: Number of calendar days advance notice required by either party to terminate this capitation arrangement without cause. Typically 90–180 days for managed care capitation contracts.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Contract Standards; State DOI Requirements

description: Timestamp when this capitation arrangement record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164.312 Audit Controls; SOC 2 Type II; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Indicates whether this capitation arrangement includes Value-Based Care (VBC) performance incentive components such as quality bonuses, shared savings, or outcome-based adjustments beyond the base PMPM.
reference: CMS Innovation Center VBC Models; NCQA HEDIS Quality Measures

description: Percentage of the PMPM rate withheld from each payment cycle pending performance or quality metric achievement. Withheld amounts are returned upon meeting contractual benchmarks. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Quality Bonus Program; NCQA HEDIS Performance Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each capitation payment disbursement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the specific capitation arrangement or rate schedule within the contract, identifying the applicable PMPM rate table, risk pool configuration, and attributed population definition.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Managed Care Plan Network Adequacy Act

description: Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Capitation payments are issued to facilities under global capitation contracts. AP voucher processing, remittance reconciliation, and financial reporting require linking each payment to the receiving

Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: Capitation payments are disbursed to group practices as the contracting entity in managed care. Payment reconciliation, 1099 reporting, and risk pool settlement require linking each payment to the gro
Reference to the health plan or benefit product under which the attributed members are enrolled. Determines the applicable benefit structure and capitation rate tier.
reference: CMS Plan Benefit Package (PBP) Standards; ACA Section 1301 QHP Requirements

description: Reference to the original capitation payment record that this record adjusts, reverses, or supplements. Populated only when prior_period_adjustment_flag is True. Enables audit trail linking of adjustment chains.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; GAAP ASC 954; Internal Audit Standards

description: Reference to the capitation contract under which this payment is made. Links to the provider contract master defining the capitation arrangement, PMPM rates, and risk-sharing terms.
reference: NAIC Model Contract Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Reference to the provider (PCP, specialist group, IPA, or ACO) receiving the capitation payment. Corresponds to the credentialed provider entity in the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).
reference: NPI Registry; CMS Provider Enrollment; NAIC Provider Standards

description: Foreign key linking to contract.vbc_contract. Business justification: capitation_payment has vbc_contract_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the payment is associated with a VBC contract. Adding vbc_contract_id creates a direct in-domain FK to vbc_contract, enabling direct query

Accounts Payable (AP) voucher or invoice number assigned in Oracle Financials when the capitation payment is posted as a payable. Links the capitation disbursement to the AP subledger for financial reporting and audit trail.
reference: GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107; Oracle Financials AP Module Standards

description: Total number of members attributed to the provider for the payment period. This is the primary driver of the gross capitation payment calculation (attributed_member_count × pmpm_rate = gross_payment_amount).
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NCQA Attribution Methodology Standards

description: Rate tier or member category applied to determine the PMPM rate for this payment (e.g., adult, pediatric, senior, ESRD, dual-eligible). Different member demographics may attract different PMPM rates under the same contract.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; CMS Medicare Advantage Rate Announcement

description: Check number or EFT/ACH trace number assigned by the financial institution for the actual disbursement of this capitation payment. Used for bank reconciliation in Oracle Financials (AP module) and provider payment dispute resolution.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation E; GAAP ASC 230

description: Timestamp when this capitation payment record was first created in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for payment processing cycles.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; SOC 2 Type II Audit Standards; Internal Data Governance Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this capitation payment record. Typically USD for domestic health insurance operations. Required for multi-currency financial reconciliation and GAAP/SAP reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP ASC 830 Foreign Currency; SAP Statement 23

description: Dollar adjustment to the gross payment resulting from retroactive enrollment changes (late enrollments, disenrollments, or eligibility corrections) identified after the initial payment calculation. Can be positive (additional payment) or negative (recoupment).
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; HIPAA 834 Transaction Standards

description: General Ledger (GL) cost center code in Oracle Financials to which this capitation payment expense is allocated. Supports financial reporting by LOB, product, and organizational unit per GAAP and SAP statutory reporting requirements.
reference: GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107; NAIC Annual Statement Instructions; Oracle Financials GL Standards

description: Total gross capitation payment amount before any adjustments, calculated as attributed_member_count multiplied by the pmpm_rate. Represents the base payment obligation for the period prior to retroactive enrollment changes, risk pool withholds, or stop-loss recoveries.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107

description: Line of Business (LOB) under which the capitation payment is made. Determines regulatory requirements, rate-setting methodology, and CMS reporting obligations applicable to the payment.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Regulations 42 CFR Part 422; CMS Medicaid Managed Care 42 CFR Part 438; ACA Section 1301

description: Final net amount disbursed to the provider after all adjustments: gross_payment_amount + enrollment_adjustment_amount - risk_pool_withhold_amount + stop_loss_recovery_amount + other_adjustment_amount. This is the actual amount reflected in the 835 ERA remittance advice.
reference: HIPAA ASC X12 835 ERA Transaction Standard; GAAP ASC 954; SAP Statement 107

description: Date on which the capitation payment was formally approved for disbursement by the authorized approver in the billing or finance system. Supports segregation of duties controls and audit trail requirements.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; SOC 1 Type II Controls; GAAP Internal Control Standards

description: Actual date the capitation payment was disbursed to the provider via EFT, check, or ACH. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and provider remittance reporting.
reference: ERISA Section 503; CMS Prompt Payment Standards; NAIC Prompt Pay Model Act

description: Mechanism by which the capitation payment was or will be disbursed to the provider. EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) and ACH (Automated Clearing House) are most common for capitation arrangements.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; CMS Electronic Payment Standards

description: Externally-visible business identifier for this capitation payment disbursement, used in provider remittance advice (835 ERA), accounts payable records, and provider portal references. Format: CAP-YYYYMM-NNNNNN.
reference: HIPAA ASC X12 835 ERA Transaction Standard; CMS Remittance Advice Standards

description: Last day of the coverage month or period for which this capitation payment is being made. Typically the last day of the calendar month in monthly capitation cycles.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards

description: First day of the coverage month or period for which this capitation payment is being made. Typically the first day of the calendar month in monthly capitation cycles.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards

description: Identifier of the capitation payment run or batch cycle that generated this payment record. Used to group all payments processed in the same cycle for reconciliation, audit, and reprocessing purposes in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
reference: Internal Payment Processing Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Current lifecycle state of the capitation payment disbursement. 'pending' = calculated but not yet approved; 'approved' = authorized for payment; 'paid' = disbursed to provider; 'voided' = cancelled before disbursement; 'reversed' = clawed back after disbursement; 'on_hold' = suspended pending investigation or contract dispute.
reference: NAIC Model Managed Care Standards; CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438

description: Classification of the capitation payment by its business purpose. 'standard' = routine monthly capitation; 'retroactive' = payment for prior periods due to enrollment changes; 'adjustment' = correction to a previously issued payment; 'stop_loss_recovery' = reimbursement under stop-loss provisions; 'risk_pool_settlement' = periodic risk pool distribution or withhold settlement.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Risk-Sharing Model Standards

description: Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate applied for this payment period, as defined in the contract rate schedule. This is the contractually negotiated amount paid per attributed member per month. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary contract pricing.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Model Managed Care Plan Standards; ERISA Section 408

description: Indicates whether this capitation payment includes or represents an adjustment to a prior payment period. True when the payment corrects or supplements a previously issued capitation payment due to retroactive enrollment changes or rate corrections.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; GAAP ASC 954 Prior Period Adjustments

description: Amount withheld from the capitation payment pending achievement of quality performance targets (e.g., HEDIS measures, STAR ratings thresholds). Released or forfeited at the end of the performance measurement period based on provider quality scores.
reference: CMS STAR Ratings Program; NCQA HEDIS Technical Specifications; CMS Medicare Advantage Quality Bonus Payment Program

description: Reference number of the 835 Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) transaction sent to the provider for this capitation payment. Used to reconcile capitation disbursements with provider accounts receivable and the EDI clearinghouse (Availity/Change Healthcare) transaction log.
reference: HIPAA ASC X12 835 ERA Transaction Standard; CMS Remittance Advice Standards

business_glossary_term: Risk-Adjusted Per Member Per Month (PMPM) Rate
description: PMPM rate after application of the risk adjustment factor (RAF score) to the base contractual PMPM rate. Reflects the risk-adjusted capitation amount per member per month for Medicare Advantage or risk-adjusted commercial arrangements.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment Methodology; 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart G; CMS Medicare Advantage Rate Announcement

description: Amount withheld from the gross capitation payment and placed into a shared risk pool per the contract terms. Risk pool withholds are held and distributed (or forfeited) at the end of the risk settlement period based on actual utilization versus targets.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Risk-Sharing Model Standards

description: Aggregate Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) score for the attributed member population used to adjust the capitation rate for this payment period. Derived from Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) coding and CMS Risk Adjustment Processing System (RAPS/EDPS) submissions.
reference: CMS Risk Adjustment Methodology; HCC Model; RAPS/EDPS Submission Standards; 42 CFR Part 422 Subpart G

description: Operational system of record from which this capitation payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and reconciliation between the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT), Billing System (HealthEdge), and Oracle Financials.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; Internal Data Governance Standards; SOC 2 Type II

description: Native identifier of this capitation payment record in the originating operational system (Facets, QNXT, or HealthEdge). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the Silver Layer and the system of record.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standards; HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164

description: Amount recovered from or credited to the provider under stop-loss provisions of the capitation contract. Positive values indicate plan reimbursement to provider for catastrophic cases exceeding the stop-loss threshold; negative values indicate provider liability adjustments.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438.6; NAIC Stop-Loss Insurance Model Act

description: Timestamp when this capitation payment record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Tracks the most recent update for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and reconciliation purposes.
reference: HIPAA Security Rule 45 CFR Part 164; SOC 2 Type II Audit Standards; Internal Data Governance Standards

description: Indicates whether this capitation payment is made under a Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) contract arrangement, as opposed to a standard fee-for-service capitation. Drives VBC performance reporting and quality incentive calculations.
reference: CMS Innovation Center VBC Models; CMS ACO Regulations 42 CFR Part 425; NCQA VBC Standards

description: Business reason code or description explaining why this capitation payment was voided or reversed. Populated only when payment_status is 'voided' or 'reversed'. Examples: contract_termination, duplicate_payment, enrollment_error, rate_correction.
reference: CMS Managed Care Regulations 42 CFR Part 438; NAIC Model Managed Care Standards; Internal Audit Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the fee schedule record in the contract domain. Primary key for the fee_schedule data product.

Foreign key linking to plan.benefit_package. Business justification: Fee schedules in health insurance are scoped to specific benefit packages — a Gold PPO benefit package carries different allowed amounts than a Bronze HMO. Claims adjudication requires selecting the c

Foreign key linking to provider.facility. Business justification: Facility-specific fee schedules (DRG base rates, per diem rates, facility fees) are standard in health insurance. Claims adjudication requires resolving the applicable fee schedule for a specific faci

Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: Group practices negotiate fee schedules that apply to all member providers. Rate configuration and claims adjudication require resolving the group-level fee schedule; fee_schedule has provider_id for
Reference to the specific health plan or benefit plan to which this fee schedule is scoped. Enables plan-level fee schedule assignment in Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration.
reference: Internal Plan Configuration Standards; CMS Plan ID Requirements

description: Reference to the provider or provider group for whom this fee schedule defines reimbursement rates. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).
reference: NPI Registry; NPPES National Plan and Provider Enumeration System

description: Reference to the provider network (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS) to which this fee schedule applies. Determines in-network vs. out-of-network reimbursement applicability.
reference: Internal Network Management Standards; CMS Network Adequacy Standards

description: Reference to the fee_schedule_id of the newer fee schedule that replaced this one when schedule_status is 'superseded'. Enables fee schedule version chain navigation and historical rate lookups.

Foreign key linking to network.tier. Business justification: Fee schedules are tier-differentiated in health insurance — Tier 1 providers receive higher reimbursement rates than Tier 2. Claims adjudication and rate lookup require knowing which tier a fee schedu

Foreign key linking to plan.year. Business justification: Fee schedules are versioned annually in health insurance — annual rate negotiations, CMS fee schedule submissions, and regulatory rate filings all require explicit plan year association. Actuarial and

Identifier for the contract amendment that introduced or modified this fee schedule version. Null for the original fee schedule. Enables traceability between fee schedule versions and contract amendments.
Indicates whether this fee schedule includes anesthesia service reimbursement, which uses base units plus time units multiplied by a conversion factor rather than standard CPT-based rates.
reference: AMA CPT Anesthesia Guidelines; CMS Anesthesia Fee Schedule

description: Dollar amount per anesthesia unit (base units + time units) used to calculate anesthesia reimbursement when anesthesia_applicable is True. Negotiated rate specific to this fee schedule.
reference: AMA CPT Anesthesia Guidelines; CMS Anesthesia Fee Schedule

description: Date on which the fee schedule was formally approved by the authorized contracting officer or governance body. Required for audit and regulatory compliance.
reference: Internal Contract Governance Policy; State DOI Filing Requirements

description: The CMS Medicare fee schedule calendar year used as the basis or benchmark for this fee schedule when fee_schedule_source is 'cms_medicare'. Enables tracking of which CMS annual update cycle the rates are derived from.
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Annual Updates; CMS IPPS Annual Rule

description: Reference to the provider contract under which this fee schedule is established. Links the fee schedule to its governing contractual agreement.

Dollar amount multiplied by the total Relative Value Units (RVUs) to calculate the reimbursement amount for RVU-based fee schedules. Applicable when payment_basis is 'rvu_based'. Sourced from CMS RBRVS conversion factor or negotiated contract rate.
reference: CMS RBRVS; AMA RVU Guidelines; CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule

description: Indicates whether Coordination of Benefits (COB) rules apply to claims adjudicated under this fee schedule. When True, COB logic determines primary vs. secondary payer responsibilities.
reference: NAIC COB Model Regulation; CMS COB Guidelines; HIPAA COB Standards

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Code Range End
description: Ending CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_start to define the scope of applicable procedure codes.
reference: AMA CPT Code Set; CMS HCPCS Level II Codes

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Code Range Start
description: Starting CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_end to define the scope of applicable procedure codes. Null if the fee schedule applies to all CPT codes or uses ICD/DRG-based scoping.
reference: AMA CPT Code Set; CMS HCPCS Level II Codes

description: Timestamp when this fee schedule record was first created in the system. Audit field capturing the initial record creation event in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Audit Policy; HIPAA Security Rule Audit Controls

description: Indicates whether this fee schedule uses Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)-based reimbursement for inpatient facility claims. When True, DRG weights and base rates govern payment rather than individual procedure codes.
reference: CMS DRG Classification System; CMS IPPS (Inpatient Prospective Payment System)

description: The negotiated base dollar rate per DRG weight unit used for inpatient facility reimbursement when drg_applicable is True. Multiplied by the DRG relative weight to calculate the total inpatient payment.
reference: CMS IPPS DRG Base Rate; CMS Medicare DRG Rates

description: Date on which the fee schedule becomes effective and applicable for claim adjudication and reimbursement calculations. Inclusive start date of the fee schedule validity period.
reference: CMS Fee Schedule Effective Date Standards; HIPAA 837 Transaction Standards

description: Free-text narrative description of the fee schedule, including its purpose, scope, special provisions, or notes relevant to adjudication staff and contract analysts.

Identifies the origin or benchmark source of the fee schedule rates: cms_medicare (based on CMS Medicare fee schedule), cms_medicaid (based on state Medicaid fee schedule), negotiated (directly negotiated with provider), custom (health plan proprietary rates), rbp_benchmark (Reference Based Pricing benchmark).
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule; CMS Medicaid Fee Schedule; Internal Contracting Policy

description: Indicates whether geographic adjustment factors (e.g., CMS Geographic Practice Cost Indices - GPCI) are applied to rates under this fee schedule. True when locality-based adjustments are in effect; False for flat national rates.
reference: CMS Geographic Practice Cost Indices (GPCI); CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule

description: Indicates whether global surgery payment rules apply under this fee schedule, bundling pre-operative, intra-operative, and post-operative services into a single reimbursement amount.
reference: CMS Global Surgery Policy; AMA CPT Global Surgery Guidelines

description: Identifies the Line of Business (LOB) to which this fee schedule applies (e.g., commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, ACA Marketplace, COBRA, FEHB). Determines which member populations and plan types are subject to this fee schedule during adjudication.
reference: CMS Medicare Advantage Regulations; ACA Marketplace Standards; NAIC LOB Classification

description: CMS Medicare locality code identifying the geographic pricing area applicable to this fee schedule when geographic_modifier_applicable is True. Used to apply GPCI adjustments to RVU-based rates.
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Locality Codes; CMS GPCI

description: Indicates whether this fee schedule applies to Medicare crossover claims where Medicare is the primary payer and the health plan is the secondary payer. Relevant for Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans.
reference: CMS Medicare Secondary Payer (MSP) Regulations; CMS Crossover Claim Guidelines

description: Indicates whether CPT or HCPCS modifiers affect reimbursement rates under this fee schedule (e.g., modifier 26 for professional component, modifier TC for technical component, modifier 50 for bilateral procedures).
reference: AMA CPT Modifier Guidelines; CMS HCPCS Modifier Codes

description: Dollar threshold above which a claim qualifies as a cost outlier, triggering additional reimbursement beyond the standard DRG or bundled payment. Applicable for institutional fee schedules with outlier payment provisions.
reference: CMS IPPS Outlier Payment Policy; CMS Medicare Outlier Regulations

description: The methodology used to calculate reimbursement amounts under this fee schedule. Options include: pct_medicare (percentage of Medicare allowable), pct_billed_charges (percentage of provider billed charges), flat_rate (fixed dollar amount per service), rvu_based (Relative Value Unit-based), bundled (bundled payment for episode of care), capitation (per-member per-month), rbp (Reference Based Pricing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pct_medicare|pct_billed_charges|flat_rate|rvu_based|bundled|capitation|rbp — promote to reference product]
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule; CMS RBRVS; AMA RVU Guidelines; CMS Bundled Payment Models

description: The percentage rate applied when payment_basis is 'pct_medicare' or 'pct_billed_charges' (e.g., 110.0000 for 110% of Medicare). Null when payment_basis is flat_rate, rvu_based, bundled, or capitation. Stored as a decimal percentage value.
reference: CMS Medicare Physician Fee Schedule; Internal Contracting Policy

description: Indicates whether Prior Authorization (PA) is required for services reimbursed under this fee schedule. When True, claims must have an approved PA on file before adjudication at the scheduled rates.
reference: CMS Prior Authorization Rules; URAC Utilization Management Standards; Internal UM Policy

description: Indicates whether a primary care physician (PCP) referral is required for services covered under this fee schedule. Applicable primarily for HMO and POS plan types.
reference: Internal Plan Design Standards; CMS HMO Regulations

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fee schedule within the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT). Used in EDI transactions and cross-system references.
reference: Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration; HIPAA EDI Standards

description: Human-readable name identifying the fee schedule (e.g., 'Commercial PPO Professional 2024', 'Medicare Advantage Institutional Q1 2024'). Used for display and reporting in Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration.
reference: Internal Naming Convention; CMS Fee Schedule Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the fee schedule. 'draft' indicates under review; 'active' indicates currently in use for adjudication; 'suspended' indicates temporarily inactive; 'terminated' indicates permanently closed; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version.

Categorizes the fee schedule by the type of services covered: professional (physician/practitioner), institutional (hospital/facility), ancillary (therapy/home health), DME (Durable Medical Equipment), lab (laboratory), or pharmacy. Drives claim adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT.
reference: CMS Fee Schedule Classification; AMA CPT Guidelines; CMS DME Fee Schedule

description: Broad clinical or administrative category of services covered by this fee schedule (e.g., 'Evaluation and Management', 'Surgery', 'Radiology', 'Pathology', 'Inpatient Facility', 'Outpatient Facility', 'Home Health', 'Behavioral Health'). Supports fee schedule segmentation and reporting. The contracted allowed amount or maximum reimbursable dollar amount for the specified procedure code, modifier, and place of service combination. This is the principal monetary value used by adjudication systems to price claims against contracted rates. Expressed in USD.

description: When rate_derivation_method is AWP_PCT, this field stores the percentage of the Average Wholesale Price (AWP) used to derive the drug or supply reimbursement rate. For example, 0.8500 represents AWP minus 15%. Commonly used in pharmacy and DME fee schedules.

description: Indicates whether this rate applies to a bilateral procedure (performed on both sides of the body). When true, the allowed amount may be adjusted per CMS bilateral surgery payment rules (150% of the unilateral rate).

description: Single episode-of-care payment amount covering all services within a defined clinical episode (e.g., joint replacement, maternity). Applicable when payment_methodology is BUNDLED. Supports Value-Based Care (VBC) and ACO payment models.

business_glossary_term: Capitation Rate Per Member Per Month (PMPM)
description: Fixed monthly payment per enrolled member paid to a provider under a capitation arrangement, regardless of services rendered. Expressed as a Per Member Per Month (PMPM) dollar amount. Applicable only when payment_methodology is CAPITATION.

description: The timestamp when this fee schedule rate record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).

description: CMS Diagnosis Related Group code used for inpatient hospital reimbursement. When the payment methodology is DRG-based, this code identifies the patient classification group that determines the base payment rate.

description: The date on which this fee schedule rate becomes effective and applicable for claims adjudication. Claims with service dates on or after this date and before the termination_date will be priced using this rate.

description: Geographic region or market area to which this rate applies. Rates may vary by state, CMS locality, metropolitan statistical area (MSA), or health plan-defined region. Expressed as a state code, CMS locality code, or internal region identifier.

description: Number of days in the global surgery period for surgical procedure codes. Defines the pre-operative and post-operative period during which related services are bundled into the surgical payment (0=endoscopic/minor, 10=minor, 90=major surgery).

description: The Line of Business (LOB) to which this fee schedule rate applies. Rates are often differentiated by LOB as Medicare, Medicaid, and commercial plans have distinct reimbursement structures and regulatory requirements.

description: The Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC) dollar amount set as the ceiling for reimbursement of a specific drug or service. When rate_derivation_method is MAC, this value represents the maximum the plan will pay regardless of billed charges.

description: Maximum number of units of this procedure code that will be reimbursed per day per member. Used to enforce utilization limits during claims adjudication and prevent overbilling.

description: Maximum number of units of this procedure code that will be reimbursed per benefit year per member. Enforces annual utilization limits during adjudication for services with frequency restrictions.

description: When rate_derivation_method is MEDICARE_PCT, this field stores the percentage of the applicable Medicare fee schedule rate used to derive the allowed amount. For example, 1.1500 represents 115% of Medicare. Used for rate benchmarking and contract analytics.

description: The reimbursement methodology governing how the provider is paid for this service. description: Daily reimbursement rate paid to a facility provider for each day of an inpatient or residential stay. Applicable when payment_methodology is PER_DIEM. May vary by level of care (e.g., ICU vs. medical/surgical).
reference: CMS Inpatient Prospective Payment System; Provider Contract Terms

description: CMS two-digit place of service code indicating the setting where the service was rendered (e.g., 11=Office, 21=Inpatient Hospital, 22=Outpatient Hospital, 23=Emergency Room). Rates may vary by place of service.

description: Foreign key linking to the member risk score record to which this HCC contributes.
The weighted coefficient applied to this specific HCC for this member-score combination, reflecting any member-level demographic or plan-type adjustments applied on top of the base HCC coefficient.
The date on which this HCC contribution became effective for the member's risk score, typically aligned to the diagnosis encounter date or CMS model effective date.

Pipe‑separated list of HCC codes contributing to the score. [Moved from member_risk_score: This pipe-separated multi-value field encodes the M:N relationship between a risk score and its contributing HCC codes in a non-relational format. With the risk_score_hcc_contribution association table in place, each HCC contribution is represented as a discrete row with proper FK linkage, making this denormalized field redundant and structurally incorrect.]
Indicates whether this HCC is the dominant driver of the member's RAF score for this scoring period. Used in RADV audit defense to identify the highest-impact diagnosis.
The RAF unit amount this HCC contributes to the member's total risk score for the payment year. Equals contribution_weight multiplied by applicable adjustment factors.
